outcome_measure: systematic bias; random error; SEM; CV; limits of agreement
instrument_protocol: Multiple retests under a named unchanged protocol; inspect additive versus proportional error and heteroscedasticity; do not treat correlation as agreement; device or protocol transfer requires new compatibility evidence
uncertainty: Formula and scale assumptions determine the uncertainty estimate
individual_response: Compatible metric-specific error can support error-exceeding change only; it does not establish causality
limitations: ABSTRACT_ONLY; reliability does not automatically transfer across athlete population device or test protocol

outcome_measure: ICC; SEM; MDC or individual change bound
instrument_protocol: Record ICC model variance components SEM formula calculation version units and confidence level; a changed device or protocol invalidates direct reuse unless compatibility is established
uncertainty: ICC depends on sample heterogeneity and SEM or MDC depends on the selected formula and confidence level
individual_response: A threshold crossing is an error-bound statement only
limitations: ABSTRACT_ONLY; different ICC and SEM formulas yield different values; no universal observation count or freshness threshold

outcome_measure: validity; reliability; sensitivity or responsiveness
instrument_protocol: Test protocol must represent the intended performance construct in the intended population and setting; protocol or equipment changes require separate evidence or explicit stratification
uncertainty: Detectable change depends on protocol-specific error relative to a separately justified worthwhile change
individual_response: Individual interpretation is limited to the validated construct and protocol
limitations: ABSTRACT_ONLY; sport population and test specificity are high; laboratory sensitivity does not guarantee competition validity

outcome_measure: typical error; confidence interval; SWC; biological variability
instrument_protocol: Use compatible baseline and follow-up protocols; record error model method and justified SWC separately; protocol or device drift blocks direct change classification unless modeled
uncertainty: Report confidence intervals and distinguish measurement error from biological response variability and worthwhile magnitude
individual_response: Display error-exceedance and worthwhile-change status separately; neither proves a stable response phenotype
limitations: SWC is decision-context dependent; threshold classification does not establish causality safety or a repeatable person-by-intervention effect

outcome_measure: measurement error; subject-by-training interaction; within-subject efficacy variation
instrument_protocol: Repeated interventions or replicated contrasts must retain a stable named measurement and training protocol; protocol changes are confounders
uncertainty: Low signal-to-noise and within-person efficacy variation remain in uncertainty even with a larger group
individual_response: A planned within-athlete comparison may be described within design limits but cannot assign a permanent responder label
limitations: ABSTRACT_ONLY; control alone may not identify within-person efficacy variation; time trend concurrent training carryover and other confounds require design handling

outcome_measure: interindividual response variance and responder classification
instrument_protocol: Classification must preserve metric protocol threshold method error model and importance threshold; incompatible studies or device protocols cannot be pooled as one personal rule
uncertainty: Threshold choice and heterogeneous error or SWC or MCID methods materially affect response rates
individual_response: Simple pre-post sign or group SD bands cannot establish within-athlete causality
limitations: Review heterogeneity and overlap limit transfer; classifications remain sensitive to method and do not establish stable personal response safety or freshness

event: variance components and replicated individual contrasts
intervention: treatment conditions in replicated crossover logic
outcome_measure: treatment-effect heterogeneity versus within-person and measurement variation
instrument_protocol: Stable outcome and intervention protocols are required across repeated contrasts; protocol drift and period effects must be modeled
uncertainty: Separate between-person variance measurement variation and within-person variation in response
individual_response: Observed between-person spread alone does not prove a repeatable personal treatment effect
limitations: Clinical rather than sport-specific; period trend autocorrelation confounding and carryover remain design threats; accumulated observations alone are noncausal

outcome_measure: confidence intervals; Bayesian specification; smallest important effect
instrument_protocol: Use transparent interval methods or a fully specified Bayesian model and justify the importance threshold independently; measurement protocol remains a separate prerequisite
uncertainty: MBI operating characteristics and claimed reduced-sample advantages are not accepted as an uncertainty model
individual_response: No likely-worthwhile label creates within-athlete causal evidence
limitations: Critique is specific to MBI; it does not reject defining an important effect but blocks reduced-sample shortcuts and universal n claims

event: MBI type-I and type-II error simulation
outcome_measure: type-I and type-II error behavior across effect and sample-size conditions
instrument_protocol: Inferential method operating characteristics must match the claim; a protocol-valid observation series remains separately required
uncertainty: MBI can inflate false-positive error under examined conditions
individual_response: A small sample has no special causal exemption and a likely-worthwhile label is not a personal-response proof
limitations: ABSTRACT_ONLY; simulation addresses MBI rather than direct athlete-monitoring thresholds freshness trend or carryover

population: patients clinicians and N-of-1 trial designers
outcome_measure: level or rate contrast with repeated assessment
instrument_protocol: Name stable outcome method and protocol across periods; model or stratify device or procedure changes; use washout transition exclusion or explicit carryover handling only when justified
uncertainty: Autocorrelation model assumptions onset transition and carryover choices determine uncertainty
individual_response: An isolated component effect is not identifiable when training adaptations overlap and cannot be separated
limitations: Clinical transfer is indirect; training carryover may be intended; period length is hypothesis-specific and accumulated data alone do not remove trend confounding or carryover

outcome_measure: operationalized outcomes; fidelity; phase data; adverse events; raw graphs
instrument_protocol: Freeze outcome definitions instrument or device method phase sequence and intervention fidelity; record every protocol deviation and version boundary
uncertainty: Measurement reliability analysis and raw phase data are required for interpretable uncertainty
individual_response: Reporting completeness alone does not establish a causal individual effect
limitations: Behavioral guidance is indirect to athlete monitoring; trend autocorrelation confounds carryover missingness and poor replication remain validity threats

event: SCED design and visual evidence standards
outcome_measure: level; trend; variability; immediacy; overlap; consistency
instrument_protocol: Operationalize measurement and preserve comparable phase protocols; interobserver agreement is required where relevant; protocol changes must be visible
uncertainty: Visual evidence must consider level trend variability immediacy overlap and consistency rather than a count alone
individual_response: Point counts cannot establish an athlete-specific causal effect without manipulation and repeated demonstrations
limitations: Education evidence-review standards are not athlete baseline or freshness rules; autocorrelation confounding carryover and measurement validity are not solved by phase counts

intervention: fixed response-guided or random baseline rules
outcome_measure: error and power under varied trend and variability
instrument_protocol: Simulation method and analysis rule must match the intended design; empirical athlete protocol compatibility is NOT_APPLICABLE
uncertainty: Error and power depend on baseline rule analysis method trend variability and simulation assumptions
individual_response: Does not permit a causal inference from a short or merely accumulated athlete series
limitations: Method- and simulation-specific; opposition to rigid stability waiting is not permission to ignore trend seasonality confounds autocorrelation or use one point

event: serially correlated level and rate contrasts
outcome_measure: level change; rate change; type-I error; power; interval width
instrument_protocol: Use a stable named outcome protocol and specify the serial correlation structure and intended level or rate contrast; device or method drift requires modeling or blocks pooling
uncertainty: Ordinary independent tests can misstate error power and interval width under serial dependence
individual_response: Keep results descriptive when serial structure cannot be supported; repeated points alone are noncausal
limitations: Model assumptions require checking; trend seasonality confounding carryover and missingness are not eliminated by a serial t-test

event: SCED design measurement baseline and analysis practices
outcome_measure: design quality; measurement; baseline sampling; visual and statistical analysis
instrument_protocol: Outcomes must be operationalized and repeatedly measured with protocol continuity and transparent measurement quality; protocol changes require explicit treatment
uncertainty: SCED quality comprises separate design measurement replication trend and analysis uncertainties
individual_response: No aggregate point count proves a personal effect
limitations: Behavioral-health directness is limited; analytic disagreement and visual-analysis reproducibility remain; autocorrelation confounds carryover and missingness require design-specific handling

event: randomized ABAB alternating-treatment multiple-baseline and changing-criterion designs
outcome_measure: visual effect-size and randomization inference
instrument_protocol: Outcome device and protocol must remain comparable and analysis must follow the actual assignment scheme; protocol changes and deviations must be recorded
uncertainty: Randomization-based uncertainty is valid only for the actual assignment scheme and pre-specified contrast
individual_response: Historical nonrandom splits receive weaker wording and explicit confounding flags
limitations: Healthcare directness is limited; randomization does not fix poor measurement trend carryover missingness or irreversibility; accumulated observations alone are noncausal

population: athletes across individual and team sports
outcome_measure: external and internal load; perception; heart rate; biochemical; neuromuscular; questionnaire; diary; psychomotor; sleep
instrument_protocol: Individualize marker selection and retain timing instrument device protocol and contextual provenance; do not pool discordant protocol versions as one signal
uncertainty: Few markers have strong evidence and no single definitive fatigue marker exists
individual_response: Discordant markers remain separate and unresolved; no readiness diagnosis or training clearance
limitations: Narrative review; mixed sports and measures; autocorrelation time trend concurrent training illness sleep environment and other confounds remain; monitoring accumulation is noncausal

event: subjective and objective wellbeing response to training load
outcome_measure: subjective wellbeing and objective marker response to acute or chronic load
instrument_protocol: Standardize timing instrument wording scoring device and protocol; preserve subjective and objective provenance and protocol changes rather than fusing streams
uncertainty: Measures often disagree and heterogeneity prevents a universal threshold or freshness rule
individual_response: Sensitivity to load is observational and does not establish within-athlete causality or safety
limitations: Systematic-review heterogeneity and possible primary-study overlap; time trend concurrent training competition illness sleep and reporting behavior remain confounds

intervention: AB reversal multiple-baseline or alternating-treatment conditioning design
outcome_measure: visual and statistical athlete-specific performance analysis
instrument_protocol: Repeated observations require a stable outcome test device and procedure; protocol changes must be modeled or treated as a boundary; multivariate context is retained
uncertainty: Serial dependency and design constraints determine inferential uncertainty
individual_response: Supports an athlete-specific prospective comparison lane only within the selected design
limitations: ABSTRACT_ONLY; methodological advocacy rather than validated universal procedure; applied constraints can weaken manipulation replication trend confound and carryover control

intervention: Practical guide to single-case experimental designs
comparator: Phase sequencing, randomization, fidelity, replication and carryover-aware alternatives
component_order: Phase sequence or multiple baseline; reversal requires reversible effects
outcome_measure: Repeated measures; procedural fidelity; phase decisions; replication
instrument_protocol: Methods guide; current Formation pilot is NOT_A_SCED and tests only whether stable repeated measurement is feasible
time_points: Repeated measures across prespecified phases in a future SCED
effect_estimate: Method guidance; no empirical pilot effect estimate
uncertainty: Publisher full text not re-opened in bounded recovery
individual_response: NOT_APPLICABLE
limitations: Not youth sport; cumulative training makes ABAB reversal unsuitable; no causal, efficacy or safety claim

component_dose: 26 reporting items covering decisions, randomization, consent, measurement, fidelity, analysis, stop reasons, raw data, adverse events and limitations
outcome_measure: Completion; stop reason; process event; procedural fidelity; adverse events; limitations
instrument_protocol: Delphi/consensus reporting guideline; current pilot borrows process fields without becoming a SCED
time_points: Repeated observations and phase events in a future SCED
missingness: Missing raw data and stop reasons must be explicit
adverse_events: Adverse events are reporting fields, not evidence that current pilot establishes safety
effect_estimate: Reporting guidance; no causal effect estimate
uncertainty: Multiple journal versions; identifiers refer to linked PMC version
individual_response: NOT_APPLICABLE
limitations: Not a conduct standard or causal validation; later SCED requires stable version, carryover/trend/autocorrelation analysis and replication

population: N-of-1 clinical methods; no athlete population
intervention: CENT 2015 explanation and elaboration for prospectively planned N-of-1 trials
comparator: Incomplete or retrospectively reconstructed period-level reporting
component_dose: Pre-specify interventions outcomes periods sequence stopping rules and analysis; exact dose is design-specific
component_order: Pre-specified randomized or otherwise justified period sequence
separation_hours: Design-specific washout or separation must address carryover
actual_interval_hours: Design-specific period duration; no universal interval
outcome_measure: Period-level intervention delivery outcomes harms withdrawals protocol deviations and missing data
instrument_protocol: CENT 2015 explanation and elaboration; companion reporting guidance
missingness: Report period-level missingness attrition and protocol deviations
adverse_events: Report harms for each intervention period
uncertainty: Carryover irreversibility period effects and measurement error remain design constraints
individual_response: NOT_APPLICABLE
limitations: Reporting guidance does not validate ABAB or crossover for cumulative training adaptation and supplies no universal baseline count freshness threshold efficacy safety or 9.5-day scientific superiority

population: general sports medicine and science measurement populations
outcome_measure: typical error and precision of reliability estimates
instrument_protocol: Repeated trials must use the same named protocol; imported error estimates require metric population method and version provenance
uncertainty: Reliability estimates require their own uncertainty
individual_response: Observed individual change must be compared with a compatible error estimate; no causal inference
limitations: ABSTRACT_ONLY; approximate participant or trial guidance is for reliability studies and is not a universal within-athlete observation gate

population: N-of-1 clinical trial designers and participants
event: prospectively planned multiple-crossover N-of-1 trial reporting
outcome_measure: participant flow; period outcomes; harms; deviations
instrument_protocol: Freeze interventions outcomes sequence periods stopping rules analysis and measurement protocol; report device or protocol deviations rather than silently combining them
uncertainty: Report period-level uncertainty and deviations under the pre-specified analysis
individual_response: Guideline supports transparent prospective comparison reporting but does not establish efficacy safety or prescription authority
limitations: OFFICIAL_FULL_TEXT_AVAILABLE_BUT_ABSTRACT_USED; reporting completeness is not design validity; onset carryover trend confounding and missingness still require separate handling

event: Delphi consensus on performance-test measurement properties and feasibility
outcome_measure: validity; reliability; responsiveness or sensitivity; interpretability; feasibility
instrument_protocol: Evaluate each test for named intended use population purpose setting method and protocol version; a generic VALID or RELIABLE label is insufficient
uncertainty: Consensus supplies a taxonomy rather than metric-specific error bounds
individual_response: Does not supply a personal baseline count or validate an individual response
limitations: Expert consensus does not validate a particular metric and provides no universal n duration or freshness threshold

population: four NCAA Division I basketball athletes
comparator: comparison among model statistic SWC CV and SEM
outcome_measure: individual CMJ performance gain classification
instrument_protocol: Use the same CMJ device test procedure timing units processing and software version across sessions; changed protocol or device blocks direct reuse unless compatibility is established
uncertainty: Jointly represent random variation error bound and separately justified worthwhile magnitude
individual_response: Narrow example may prototype an error-aware report but does not validate a stable response or causal attribution
limitations: ABSTRACT_ONLY; four athletes one outcome and one cadence; five sessions biweekly and CV preference are application details not defaults; trend learning concurrent training and carryover remain confounds

event: weekly reactive-strength testing across baseline and intervention
outcome_measure: reactive strength outcome family
instrument_protocol: Weekly reactive-strength test protocol; device method and calculation version were not extracted in the search packet and remain NOT_VERIFIED; any change would require explicit stratification
uncertainty: Participant-level plots or estimates and combined versus individual interpretations are required
individual_response: Demonstrates individual paths and sport SCED feasibility; does not establish general efficacy safety or a universal threshold
limitations: Four cases and nonconcurrent baselines; history concurrent training serial dependence trend learning maturation and intervention carryover remain concerns; phase durations are application details not product defaults

intervention: Cochrane guidance for summary-of-findings tables and certainty assessment
comparator: Unstructured certainty language without explicit domains or outcome-specific judgments
component_dose: Outcome-specific certainty appraisal across risk of bias inconsistency indirectness imprecision publication bias and applicable upgrading considerations
outcome_measure: Outcome-specific certainty and reasons for rating decisions
instrument_protocol: Cochrane Handbook chapter 14 and GRADE framework
missingness: Unavailable studies inaccessible full text and missing outcome data must remain visible in certainty judgments
adverse_events: Adverse outcomes require their own outcome-specific appraisal when reported
uncertainty: Certainty may differ by outcome; imprecision inconsistency indirectness and bias require explicit judgment
individual_response: NOT_APPLICABLE
limitations: Generic review guidance does not supply athlete-specific transfer efficacy safety minimum n freshness thresholds or automated-prescription authority; adolescent 800 and 1500 m population and 9.5-day intervention directness must be judged separately
